summary: Applied the 'luxe' (Luxora) dark UI across the public-facing pages while keeping 100% of logic/i18n and avoiding conflicts. Built a shared, page-scoped design system (src/components/luxe/theme.ts + primitives.tsx, plus additive .luxe-* utility classes in globals.css that never override the global light theme used by admin/staff/dashboard). Restyled the marketplace homepage components (Header, HeroSection, StatsStrip, LiveAuctionGrid, AuctionCard, MarketSections, SellCTA, Footer, FeaturedLotCard, SectionHeader dark tone) to near-black + warm gold with Playfair headings, wired the real marketplace homepage to /browse (removed the accidental redirect), and converted the shared TopNav + ProductAuctionCard, the list pages (live/upcoming/results), the auction detail page (/auctions/[id], styling only), and storefront (switched from the buyer CollectorShell to public TopNav + luxe). Fixed search/breadcrumb redirects that pointed to '/' (now /luxe) to point to /browse. All files pass lint and the dev server compiles cleanly.
current_status: Public pages now share a consistent luxe dark theme. Not yet converted (kept light to avoid breaking): buyer/seller shell (DashboardLayout/CollectorShell) and its pages (dashboard, wallet, earnings, inventory, won-items, profile, kyc, watchlist, post-item), staff/admin areas, auth, and auction-room realtime page. Embedded components on the auction detail page (AuctionResultBanner, PurchaseContractPanel, CountdownTimer, StatusBadge) retain their own styling and may need a follow-up pass. Frontend (localhost:3000) and backend (localhost:8096) dev servers running.
